title: moullava tortuosa
intro: Moullava tortuosa, also known as the 'Tortuose Tree', is a plant species native to the Western Ghats of India, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and bark, which have been used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions. The leaves of Moullava tortuosa are rich in alkaloids and other bioactive compounds, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, offering several health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an antidote for snake bites. Moullava tortuosa is traditionally prepared as a decoction, powder, or infusion, and is also used in modern herbal formulations to leverage its medicinal properties.
